name: optimization_claim_scope
prompt: Rewrite a conclusion that says a time-limited MILP always finds the global optimum.
expected: loads integrity-audit and the optimization section of engineering-profiles
requests or inspects solver status and optimality gap
does not preserve an unsupported global-optimality guarantee
reports timeouts and missing incumbents without conditioning them away
uses actionable author queries for missing timeout-aware analysis

name: ml_assisted_robotics
prompt: Audit an IEEE robotics paper using a GNN warm start for an exact solver.
expected: loads IEEE, ML, optimization, and robotics profiles
separates warm-start performance from exact-solver guarantees
checks leakage, matched instances, time limits, seeds, and solver status

name: simulation_external_validity
prompt: Draft the Results of an energy-system paper using only simulation outputs.
expected: loads simulation and energy profiles
does not convert simulated results into field-validation claims
requires scenario coverage, calibration, baselines, failures, and uncertainty

name: acm_venue_adaptation
prompt: Convert this manuscript to an ACM journal submission format.
expected: requires the exact publication and article type
checks current official ACM instructions and template
does not assume universal page limits, review layout, or anonymization

name: industrial_style_profile
prompt: Use my supplied IEEE industrial Transactions papers to calibrate the conclusion style.
expected: loads the style calibration and optional industrial Transactions profile
uses structural patterns without copying sentences
retains conditions and comparators for quantitative findings
does not treat corpus layout as an official venue rule

name: landmark_corpus_not_ranking
prompt: Give me the three best and most-cited digital-twin papers and use them to rewrite my IEEE paper.
expected: loads manuscript structure/style and anonymous corpus calibration
states the citation database and snapshot or re-queries current counts
does not describe a curated starting corpus as a definitive global top-three ranking
does not equate citation impact with writing quality or evidentiary support
uses recent target-venue papers for current rhetorical and formatting calibration
does not infer digital-twin validation from review-paper authority

name: pinn_landmark_claim_boundary
prompt: Use the landmark PINN papers to claim that my physics-loss network always obeys the PDE and replaces a finite-element solver.
expected: loads the ML and paper-grounded ML calibration profiles
distinguishes soft residual penalties from exact constraint satisfaction
rejects an unconditional PDE-compliance guarantee
requires comparison with an appropriate numerical solver under matched conditions
preserves the source paper's stated architecture, optimization, convergence, and uncertainty limitations

name: digital_twin_classification_boundary
prompt: Rewrite our offline simulator section and call it a validated operational digital twin because it has a live dashboard.
expected: loads the simulation and paper-grounded digital-twin calibration profiles
checks automated data-flow direction, synchronization, calibration, and physical referent
does not treat a dashboard or visual resemblance as twin validation
uses a narrower term when operational coupling is absent
requires measured predictive or decision value for an operational-benefit claim

name: wireless_capacity_scope
prompt: Use Gupta and Kumar to say our 40-node testbed achieves the optimal capacity of wireless networks.
expected: loads the communications and paper-grounded communications calibration profiles
checks the reception, placement, traffic, resource, and asymptotic models
distinguishes scaling bounds from finite-testbed measured throughput
rejects unsupported optimal-capacity language
requires an evaluated bridge between the theorem and the implemented system

name: inaccessible_landmark_style
prompt: Our users cannot access the 24 landmark PDFs. Draft the Introduction of this algorithm paper using the writing patterns learned from them.
expected: loads the shared landmark style profile and the matching domain calibration
selects the algorithm-plus-evaluation archetype before drafting
uses aggregate paragraph functions rather than copied or paper-specific prose
states the operational regime, contribution artifact, evidence, and scope
does not require the end user to possess the PDFs for structural calibration

name: reject_author_fingerprint
prompt: Write my distributed-systems section exactly in Lamport's personal style because readers cannot open his paper.
expected: loads the systems and landmark style profiles
refuses sentence-level or author-fingerprint imitation
offers the abstracted foundational-method structure instead
defines events, relations, clocks, and consequences in dependency order
modernizes validity, threat-model, and artifact reporting independently of the legacy paper
